title: Site Hierarchy Inventory
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:site:topology`. from the `site_topology` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/topology/site-topology`. Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per site per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `siteId` (string), `siteName` (string), `siteType` (string), `parentSiteName` (string). CIM data model: Compute_Inventory.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:site:topology" | stats dc(siteId) as total_sites values(siteType) as types by parentSiteName | sort parentSiteName
description: Provides a complete inventory of the Catalyst Center site hierarchy (areas, buildings, floors) for infrastructure documentation and capacity planning.
value: Understanding the site hierarchy is foundational for location-based analytics. This inventory ensures Splunk has a complete map of the physical infrastructure.
implementation: Enable the `site_topology` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA calls `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/topology/site-topology` on a **3600s** (1 hour) default interval. Key fields: `siteId`, `siteType`, `siteName`, `parentSiteName`.
visualization: Hierarchical or indented table (parentSiteName, total_sites, types), tree or Sankey if exported to a viz app, single value of global site count in a subsearch if needed.
knownFalsePositives:
**Recently added building or floor appearing as new inventory.** After a legitimate site hierarchy expansion, new sites appear in the inventory. Distinguish by correlating with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs"` for site creation audit entries by an authorized administrator. Suppress by allowing 24 hours after a site creation audit entry before flagging the new site as unexpected.

**Site hierarchy reorganization moving devices between sites.** When the site hierarchy is restructured (e.g., moving a floor from one building to another), the inventory shows the site disappearing from one location and appearing in another. Distinguish by checking whether the `siteId` UUID remains the same but its parent path changed. Do not suppress — the reorganization should be tracked, but it should be categorized as a move rather than an anomaly.

**Global site appearing with zero devices after initial Catalyst Center setup.** The Global site and its default children (e.g., Unassigned) always exist in Catalyst Center but may have zero devices. These are expected structural elements. Distinguish by checking whether the site's `name` is "Global" or a default site. Suppress by filtering default structural sites from capacity planning views.

**Site count increasing after Catalyst Center upgrade adds new default sites.** Some Catalyst Center upgrades add new default site hierarchy elements or policy domains. These appear as new sites in the inventory. Distinguish by checking whether new sites appeared coincident with a Catalyst Center upgrade. Suppress by establishing a new baseline after the upgrade.

references.title: Catalyst Center Site Topology API — Cisco DevNet

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) ≥1.0 with the **site** modular input enabled, writing to `index=catalyst` with `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:site`.
• Catalyst Center **2.3.5+** with the site hierarchy configured (Areas, Buildings, Floors).
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE** for site hierarchy read access.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
The TA polls `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/site` at the configured interval (default 3600 seconds / 1 hour). Each event represents one site hierarchy entry (Area, Building, or Floor).

Key fields for site hierarchy inventory:
• `name`: site name (e.g., "HQ Building A", "Floor 3").
• `siteId`: unique site UUID.
• `siteHierarchy`: full hierarchy path (e.g., "Global/US/New York/HQ Building A/Floor 3").
• `siteType`: "area", "building", or "floor".
• `parentId`: parent site UUID — defines the hierarchy tree.
• `latitude`, `longitude`: geo-coordinates (if configured).

Expected volume: ~1 event per site per poll. A deployment with 50 buildings averaging 3 floors each = ~200 events/poll + area entries.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:site"
| stats dc(siteId) as total_sites dc(eval(if(siteType="building",siteId,null()))) as buildings dc(eval(if(siteType="floor",siteId,null()))) as floors dc(eval(if(siteType="area",siteId,null()))) as areas
| eval summary="Sites: ".total_sites.", Areas: ".areas.", Buildings: ".buildings.", Floors: ".floors
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`dc(eval(if(siteType="building",siteId,null())))`**: Counts distinct building-type sites. The `eval/if` pattern enables counting by type within a single `stats` command.
• **`dc(siteId) as total_sites`**: Total unique sites in the hierarchy — the denominator for capacity planning.
• This search provides a point-in-time snapshot of the site hierarchy. For change detection, see UC-5.13.52.

For a full hierarchy listing: `| table siteHierarchy, siteType, name, siteId | sort siteHierarchy` — produces a tree-style listing of the entire hierarchy.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Design > Network Hierarchy. Count the areas, buildings, and floors visible in the hierarchy tree. Compare with the Splunk totals.
• **Spot check:** Pick 3-5 sites and verify that `siteHierarchy`, `siteType`, and `name` match the UI representation.
• **Geographic data:** If latitude/longitude are configured, verify a few buildings against known coordinates.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Site hierarchy tree visualization (or table sorted by `siteHierarchy`), summary tiles for total areas/buildings/floors, and a map visualization for sites with geo-coordinates.
• **Inventory baseline:** Capture a weekly snapshot of site counts for capacity planning and growth tracking.
• **Integration:** Use site hierarchy data as enrichment for other Catalyst Center UCs — join `siteId` to resolve site names and locations.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Site count lower than expected:** The TA may not be paging through all sites. Check for pagination errors in `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd "TA_cisco_catalyst"`.
• **Hierarchy path truncated:** Some Catalyst Center versions truncate very deep hierarchies. Verify with the API directly if the TA shows incomplete paths.
• **Stale site data:** The site input polls hourly by default. Recent site changes may not appear until the next poll cycle.
• **No site topology events arriving:** verify the `site_topology` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
• **Missing or empty values in site hierarchy:** newly created sites may have empty parent references until the hierarchy is fully configured in Catalyst Center.
• **Fewer records than expected:** check the service account role; credential access denied errors indicate insufficient permissions.
grandmaExplanation: We map out your entire network site structure — areas, buildings, and floors — so every device and problem can be tied to a physical location.
